nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code project settings file listing enabled plugins for the local development assistant.

nodes.name: .config.kiro
nodes.summary: Marker/config file for the Kiro spec-driven development tool tied to the user-authentication feature spec.

nodes.name: requirements.md
nodes.summary: Requirements document for a user-authentication feature (signup, OTP verification, login, JWT token management, auth UI, session persistence/logout, and user data storage) written in EARS acceptance-criteria format.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern list controlling which files and directories the Understand-Anything knowledge graph scanner excludes from analysis.

nodes.name: capacitor.config.ts
nodes.summary: Capacitor configuration for packaging the NowCart web client as a native mobile app, defining app ID, name, and web directory settings.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: ESLint flat-config for the client codebase, defining linting rules and plugin setup for TypeScript/React source files.

nodes.name: gen-icons.mjs
nodes.summary: Node build script that generates app icon assets (favicons/mobile icons) for the client from a source image.

nodes.name: nginx.conf
nodes.summary: Nginx server configuration used to serve the built client SPA and proxy API requests in production/container deployment.

nodes.name: schema.d.ts
nodes.summary: Auto-generated TypeScript type definitions describing the backend API's request/response schema, used for typed API client calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Large generated declaration file (868 lines), likely produced by an OpenAPI-to-TypeScript generator.

nodes.name: Button.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable styled Button UI component supporting variants, sizes, loading state, and icon slots.

nodes.name: Button
nodes.summary: React functional component rendering a button element with variant/size/loading/icon props, composing class names via the cn utility.

nodes.name: cn.ts
nodes.summary: Tiny utility module exporting a class-name concatenation helper used across UI components.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Joins truthy class name fragments into a single space-separated string, similar to the classnames/clsx pattern.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet defining base styles, CSS custom properties (design tokens like shadows/colors), and Tailwind layer imports for the client app.

nodes.name: SosPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Minimal placeholder page component for an SOS/emergency-help route in the client app.

nodes.name: SosPage
nodes.summary: Trivial React page component stub for the SOS page route.

nodes.name: Card.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable Card UI component providing surface styling, padding variants, and optional interactive hover states.

nodes.name: Card
nodes.summary: React functional component rendering a styled card container with configurable padding and interactive hover/shadow behavior.

nodes.name: Chip.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable Chip/badge UI component with tone and size variants, optionally rendering a leading icon.

nodes.name: Chip
nodes.summary: React functional component rendering a pill-shaped chip with tone-based coloring and optional icon.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the client app, defining plugins, dev server settings, and build output options.

nodes.name: add_5000_more.py
nodes.summary: Data-generation utility script that appends roughly 5000 additional product/catalog records, verifying associated image URLs before inclusion.

nodes.name: verify_image
nodes.summary: Validates that a catalog row's image URL is reachable and returns a real image by issuing an HTTP request and checking content length/headers.

nodes.name: check_cors.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic script for verifying CORS headers/behavior of the NowCart backend API.

nodes.name: check_page2.py
nodes.summary: Small ad-hoc script for inspecting/testing a second page of paginated data or results, likely used during catalog debugging.

nodes.name: create_verified_csv.py
nodes.summary: Data-pipeline script that builds a CSV of catalog records after verifying each entry's image URL is valid and reachable.

nodes.name: check_image_url
nodes.summary: Checks whether a given image URL responds successfully via an HTTP HEAD/GET-style request before a row is written to the verified CSV.

nodes.name: find_placeholders.py
nodes.summary: Scans catalog data rows to detect placeholder or invalid product images by comparing downloaded content size against expected thresholds.

nodes.name: check_size
nodes.summary: Fetches a row's image URL and inspects the response content-length header to flag likely placeholder images.

nodes.name: fix_placeholders.py
nodes.summary: Remediation script that re-checks and replaces placeholder/broken product images identified in the catalog data.

nodes.name: check_real_image
nodes.summary: Verifies whether a catalog row's image URL points to a genuine (non-placeholder) image by inspecting HTTP response size and headers.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker file establishing the server.app Python package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package docstring/init module describing the async_jobs architecture: API endpoint to SQS queue to Lambda function to DynamoDB result, with frontend polling for completion, used to offload heavy operations like vision analysis and cart decomposition.

nodes.name: lambda_handlers.py
nodes.summary: AWS Lambda handler stubs for offloaded async jobs — vision analysis, share/decomposition, and outcome processing — invoked via SQS to scale heavy operations outside the main API process.

nodes.name: vision_handler
nodes.summary: Lambda entry point that processes vision-analysis job events from SQS, parses payloads, runs analysis, and returns/logs results with error handling per item.

nodes.name: share_handler
nodes.summary: Lambda entry point handling share/cart-decomposition job events from SQS, parsing payloads and aggregating per-item results with error handling.

nodes.name: outcome_handler
nodes.summary: Lambda entry point processing outcome-tracking job events from SQS, parsing payloads and recording per-item success/failure results.
